purpose: The four gate tiers every grammar-affecting decision is routed through, derived from the GrammarProblems finding that a general LLM is unreliable on naḥw. A decision object MUST declare a gate at least as strict as its triggers require; validate_linguistic_decisions.py enforces this.
principle: General LLM confidence is not evidence. A correct conclusion with wrong iʿrāb reasoning is unsafe. Prefer pending over wrong.

auto_safe.note: the only tier that may auto-author a hover gloss without a second check

two_vote_required.requires: two independent checks that AGREE on BOTH the conclusion AND the reasoning
two_vote_required.note: the conclusion alone is insufficient; the iʿrāb reason must also match

human_source_review_required.requires: owner/human + source-address evidence before any apply

never_auto_resolve.action: reject — never ship; quarantine or pending with the precise blocker

decision_object_contract.gate_must_be: >= the strictest tier any trigger requires
decision_object_contract.reject_if: gate weaker than triggers, OR reasoning absent on a two_vote/irab decision, OR any never_auto trigger present
